description: Rebalance worker Phase 2 — sharded leader election. Closes ROADMAP P2 'Rebalance worker not sharded'. Mirrors gc/lifecycle Phase 2: per-shard leases `rebalance-leader-0..N-1` driven by STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS (default 1, range [1, 1024]). Worker registered SkipLease=true; ShardedFanOut spawns one goroutine per shard. meta.Store.ListBucketsShard returns disjoint bucket subset per shard via fnv32a(bucketID) % totalShards. ProgressTracker aggregates per-shard snapshots; /drain-progress returns merged counts (operator UX unchanged). Single-replica SHARDS=1 reproduces Phase 1 byte-for-byte. lab-tikv-3 bench measures ~3× throughput at SHARDS=3 vs SHARDS=1. 5 stories.

userStories.title: meta.Store.ListBucketsShard + shard-scoped bucket distribution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a meta API that returns only buckets belonging to a specific shard so multiple worker replicas can scan disjoint subsets.
acceptanceCriteria: New method `meta.Store.ListBucketsShard(ctx context.Context, shardID int, totalShards int) ([]*meta.Bucket, error)` returns buckets where `fnv32a(bucket.ID) % totalShards == shardID`
Validation: `shardID ∈ [0, totalShards)` AND `totalShards >= 1`; out-of-range returns new `ErrInvalidShard` sentinel
Memory backend: iterate in-mem map, filter by hash

Cassandra backend: stream via existing `ListBuckets` paging, filter in-process (no schema change — buckets table partition key is `name`; secondary index by shard not worth it for operator-driven scan rate)
TiKV backend: scan key prefix `s/bk/`, filter by hash on each row (existing prefix scan shape)

New contract test `caseListBucketsShard` in `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go` covers all three backends: seed 100 buckets with random UUIDs → list with 3 shards → assert union covers all 100 buckets exactly once (no duplicates, no missing) + ~33/33/33 split (5% tolerance)
Unit test: 1000 fake bucket IDs distributed across 3 shards → ~33/33/33 (5% tolerance); 10 shards → ~10% each (10% tolerance)
Edge case: `totalShards=1` → returns all buckets (matches existing `ListBuckets` behavior with shardID=0); contract test asserts this
Edge case: `shardID == totalShards` → ErrInvalidShard (off-by-one guard)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Rebalance worker fan-out — per-shard leases + SkipLease=true
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the rebalance worker to fan out per-shard via the existing supervisor SkipLease pattern so multiple replicas can run shards concurrently.
acceptanceCriteria: `cmd/strata/workers/rebalance.go` register changes `SkipLease: false` → `SkipLease: true` — worker owns its own leader election

Build constructor reads `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS` env at construct time (default 1, range [1, 1024]; out-of-range clamped + WARN-logged via `deps.Logger`); pattern mirrors `clampShards` from `cmd/strata/workers/gc.go`

New `internal/rebalance.ShardedFanOut` struct mirrors `internal/gc/fanout.go` field shape: `Name`, `Locker`, `ShardCount`, `Build func(shardID int) *Worker`, `LeaderTTL`, `LeaderRenew`, `AcquireRetry`, `OnLeader func(acquired bool)`, `Logger`, `holders map[int]bool` + RWMutex
`ShardedFanOut.Run(ctx)` spawns one goroutine per shard ID `0..shards-1` via `wg.Go`; each goroutine runs `runShardOnce` in a backoff loop mirroring `gc.FanOut.superviseShard`
`runShardOnce` acquires `rebalance-leader-<shardID>` lease via `leader.Session.AwaitAcquire`; on acquire calls `Build(shardID).RunShard(workCtx, shardID, shardCount)`; releases lease on exit

Per-shard panic recovery: increment `strata_worker_panic_total{worker="rebalance",shard="<i>"}` counter + log ERROR + restart on exponential backoff (1s → 5s → 30s → 2m, reset after 5 min healthy) — same shape as gc fan-out

`OnLeader` callback folded: tracks shard count owned by this replica via atomic counter; emit `(worker="rebalance", acquired=true)` ONCE when counter goes 0→1; emit `(worker="rebalance", acquired=false)` ONCE when counter goes 1→0; no events on intermediate transitions
Worker Build constructor returns a Runner that wraps the `ShardedFanOut` (Runner.Run delegates to FanOut.Run)

Unit test: fake locker + ShardedFanOut with 3 shards → assert 3 lease acquires (one per shard); cancel ctx → assert 3 releases; simulate panic in `runShardOnce` for shard 1 → assert backoff + restart + panic_total metric incremented + sibling shards unaffected
Unit test: leader-event folding — 3 shards acquired sequentially produce exactly 1 `acquired=true` event; release all 3 produces exactly 1 `acquired=false` event
Integration test against memory backend: 3-shard fan-out + 9 buckets seeded → each shard handles its own subset (verified via worker scan log inspection)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-shard worker iteration + sharded ProgressTracker merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the worker iteration to scan only its shard's bucket subset AND the ProgressTracker to aggregate per-shard contributions into a single per-cluster snapshot for the admin API.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/rebalance.Worker.RunShard(ctx context.Context, shardID, totalShards int) error` — same iteration loop as today but iterates only `meta.Store.ListBucketsShard(ctx, shardID, totalShards)` instead of full `ListBuckets`
Iteration span name unchanged (`worker.rebalance.tick`); attributes carry `strata.rebalance.shard=<i>` + existing `strata.worker="rebalance"`

`ProgressTracker.CommitScan(...)` signature extended to accept `shardID int`; per-cluster snapshot store widens from `progressCache map[clusterID]ProgressSnapshot` → `progressCache map[clusterID]map[shardID]ProgressSnapshot`

`ProgressTracker.Snapshot(clusterID)` returns merged snapshot — sums Migratable/StuckSinglePolicy/StuckNoPolicy/Bytes across all shard contributions; takes latest `LastScanAt` (max across shards); merges per-bucket maps (each bucket lives in exactly one shard, so no key collision)
`/drain-progress` admin endpoint reads merged snapshot — operator-facing wire shape unchanged
`/drain-impact` admin endpoint's synchronous-scan path still triggers full `ListBuckets` (NOT sharded) — it's a one-shot admin scan, not a worker per-tick; documented in code comment

Completion detection (from drain-cleanup US-005) fires once when SUM across ALL shards transitions to 0 — track `mergedCompletionFiredAt` at the per-cluster level (not per-shard) so multiple shards hitting 0 simultaneously don't cause double-fire
Unit test: 3 shards write per-shard snapshots `{Migratable:10}, {Migratable:20}, {Migratable:30}` → MergedSnapshot returns `Migratable:60`

Unit test: 3 shards transition sum from >0 to 0 across multiple ticks → completion fires EXACTLY once; new chunks land on one shard then drain again → completion fires twice (regression coverage for the idempotent-but-replayable contract)
Unit test: byBucket maps from per-shard snapshots merge correctly (no duplicate keys since each bucket lives in exactly one shard)

Integration test (memory backend, simulated multi-shard scan): seed buckets with chunks on a draining cluster, run scan from 3 shards, assert MergedSnapshot matches the full-scan result from Phase 1 (golden file or computed parity)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: lab-tikv-3 multi-leader bench + published numbers
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a measured bench against the lab-tikv-3 stack proving the shard fan-out gives roughly linear throughput improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: New bench harness `scripts/bench-rebalance-multi.sh` mirrors `scripts/multi-replica-smoke.sh` shape
Spins up `lab-tikv-3` compose profile (existing 3-replica TiKV-backed stack via `--profile lab-tikv-3`)
Plants N=1000 buckets each with 10 chunks = 10k chunks on cluster `default` via aws-cli PUT loop with admin credentials
Seeds buckets with `Placement={default:1, cephb:1}` mode=weighted (multi-cluster policy so all chunks are migratable on drain)
Drains `default` evacuate mode; measures wall-clock from `POST /admin/v1/clusters/default/drain {mode:evacuate}` until `chunks_on_cluster=0` via `/drain-progress` polling
Runs once with `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS=1` (baseline) + once with `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS=3`; prints two timings + ratio + verdict (`SPEEDUP_OK` or `SPEEDUP_FAILED`)

Expected: SHARDS=3 wall-clock ≤ 40% of SHARDS=1 wall-clock (≥2.5× speedup); not strictly 3× because each replica also competes for the same target cluster's RADOS write bandwidth — script EXITS NON-ZERO if SHARDS=3 wall-clock > 70% of SHARDS=1 (regression guard)
New `make bench-rebalance-multi` Makefile target wraps the script

Bench docs published at `docs/site/content/architecture/benchmarks/rebalance.md` — table with SHARDS column vs wall-clock seconds + curve summary; reference back to gc/lifecycle Phase 2 benchmark for comparison context
Numbers captured in ROADMAP close-flip narrative (US-005)
`go vet ./...` passes (bench script is bash; seeding harness uses existing aws-cli flow, no new Go binary)

userStories.title: Smoke + docs + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As an operator and as a future-maintainer, I need every change validated end-to-end against the lab + documented so I trust the cycle is actually done.
acceptanceCriteria: New `scripts/smoke-rebalance-scale.sh` covers four scenarios per-step `echo "==> Scenario X: ..."`

Scenario A (single-replica fan-out): start single strata replica with `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS=3`; verify worker boots 3 shard goroutines (grep log `rebalance.*starting` with shard=0,1,2 inside one process); seed 30 buckets; drain a cluster evacuate; assert migration completes; assert EXACTLY 1 `leader_for=rebalance acquired=true` heartbeat event (folded — verified via worker_locks + UI cluster_nodes row inspection)

Scenario B (lab-tikv-3 multi-leader): start lab-tikv-3 (3 replicas) with `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS=3`; verify 3 leases distributed (one per replica via Cassandra `worker_locks` table inspection — each holder different); seed 30 buckets; drain a cluster evacuate; assert each replica's worker log shows scans only for `shardID == myShardID`
Scenario C (back-compat SHARDS=1): start single replica with `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS=1`; verify exactly 1 `rebalance-leader-0` lease; iteration log identical to legacy single-leader scan

Scenario D (replica failover): kill 1 replica of lab-tikv-3; surviving 2 replicas acquire the freed lease within `STRATA_GC_LEASE_TTL`; bench continues; on bring-up of killed replica, ownership rebalances within next acquisition cycle
`make smoke-rebalance-scale` Makefile target wraps the script
Script EXITS NON-ZERO on any failure

`docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` gains new `Multi-leader scaling` subsection: `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS` env doc + lab-tikv-3 bench reference + ownership distribution explanation
`docs/site/content/architecture/benchmarks/rebalance.md` (created in US-004) referenced from operator runbook

Project root `CLAUDE.md` `Background workers` section gets updated rebalance bullet: describes the new fan-out (`SkipLease: true` + per-shard leases + `STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS` env + folded heartbeat chip)

`ROADMAP.md` close-flip the P2 entry → `~~**P2 — Rebalance worker not sharded — single goroutine bottleneck on large deploys.**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary referencing the lab-tikv-3 bench result and STRATA_REBALANCE_SHARDS env>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`; closing SHA backfilled in follow-up commit on main
`tasks/prd-rebalance-scale-phase-2.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule)
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
`pnpm run build` succeeds
`make smoke-rebalance-scale` passes
`make bench-rebalance-multi` runs to completion (may take >5min, OK)
Typecheck passes
